description_long: This sculpture depicts the Hindu deity Ganesha, characterized by an elephant head with a curved trunk and large ears, and a human body adorned with traditional jewelry and a draped garment. The figure stands on a rectangular plinth, with one leg slightly forward, suggesting movement or dance. The right arm is raised, and the left arm is bent, holding an indistinct object. The surface is textured, with visible details in the jewelry, garment folds, and facial features. The sculpture is rendered in a gray stone-like material, giving it a solid and weighty appearance. The overall form is robust, with rounded contours and a sense of dynamic posture.
description: A gray stone sculpture of the Hindu deity Ganesha, featuring an elephant head and human body in a dynamic, dancing pose with detailed jewelry and draped clothing.
description_view_0: Front view showing Ganesha's trunk, raised right arm, and detailed garment folds, with the left leg stepping forward.
description_view_1: Three-quarter view highlighting the curvature of the trunk, the raised right arm, and the left arm holding an object behind the body.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizing the profile of the elephant head, the rounded belly, and the intricate jewelry on the arms and neck.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the head, the draped garment, and the detailed hair or headdress, with both arms visible in their raised positions.
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the background is isolated and does not indicate a particular setting.
